module.name: client
module.purpose: Client crate for the user-facing session. event.rs defines ClientEvent, the user-facing event surface; session.rs implements ClientSession, a self-connecting client that mirrors HostSession's worker + shutdown model. new() builds the user ClientEvent channel via build_channel(), initializes agent_tx as an empty SharedNetSender<SessionEvent> (Arc<Mutex<None>>), leaves worker None, and creates a fresh shutdown Arc<AtomicBool>. connect() takes no parameters and spawns the worker via the private spawn_worker (cloning the shared agent_tx, the user_tx, and the shutdown flag), storing the returned JoinHandle in worker. spawn_worker loads the global Config, parses config.client.core_address into a SocketAddr, and loops while not shut down: it connect_channel::<SessionEvent>(address) to the agent (retrying on connect error), stores the returned NetSender into the shared agent_tx, and runs run_worker on the returned NetReceiver; run_worker builds a current-thread tokio runtime and block_on's an async loop that recv()s each inbound SessionEvent, translates it via the private to_client_event, and sends any resulting ClientEvent through user_tx, breaking when shutdown is set. create_task builds a TaskSignature { name: request, id: TaskId::new() } (the client allocates the task id) and sends SessionEvent::Task(signature, TaskEvent::Create { request }) to the agent via the private send_to_agent, while cancel_task builds a TaskSignature keyed by the given TaskId and sends SessionEvent::Task(signature, TaskEvent::Cancel); send_to_agent locks the shared agent_tx (std::sync::Mutex, poison-tolerant) and try_sends the event when a connection is present. to_client_event maps only content-bearing inbound task events to ClientEvent::Common(String) - TaskEvent::Status(TaskStatus::Update { content }), TaskEvent::Status(TaskStatus::Failed { reason }), TaskEvent::Preview { content }, and TaskEvent::CreateFailed { reason } - and returns None for lifecycle-only task statuses (Started/Canceled/Succeed) and all execution events, so they are not surfaced. receiver() borrows the user ClientEvent receiver end (&Receiver<ClientEvent>) so the caller (for example the CLI) can consume it with recv(), and close() sets the shutdown Arc<AtomicBool> flag (Ordering::Relaxed) and, if present, takes and joins the worker JoinHandle. All struct fields are private (agent_tx, user_tx, user_rx, worker, shutdown). client/Cargo.toml now depends on tokio (net, rt-multi-thread, sync, time) to drive the current-thread recv runtime; lib.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only.
